name: Hilton Jefferson
abstract:
Hilton Jefferson (July 30, 1903 – November 14, 1968) was an American jazz alto saxophonist born in Danbury, Connecticut, United States, perhaps best known for leading the saxophone section from 1940 to 1949 in the Cab Calloway band. Jefferson is said to have been "a soft, delicate saxophone player, with an exquisite sensibility."
In 1929…

about_quotes.speaker: Big Nick Nicholas
about_quotes.evidence: I was so glad to meet Hilton Jefferson and make some records with him, later on.

about_quotes.speaker: Hinton Milt
about_quotes.evidence: Hilton Jefferson my dear friend is helping me with the two o‟clock feeding.
about_quotes.doc_title: Hinton Milt — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: David Albert Francis
about_quotes.evidence: I noticed guys like Jonah Jones and Milt Henton and Keg Johnson and Hilton Jefferson, these guys, how they would run to the Western Union and send that money home first... And saxophones were Hilton Jefferson, Sam Taylor, Charlie Frazier, Chops -- Al Gibson.

about_quotes.speaker: Walter P. Thomas
about_quotes.evidence: We had another guy on baritone... There was Hilton Jefferson, Walter Thomas, Andrew Brown...

about_quotes.speaker: Patricia Willard
about_quotes.evidence: I also worked a replacement for Hilton Jefferson on the eastern coast with Duke.

about_quotes.speaker: Budd Johnson
about_quotes.evidence: Now Hilton Jefferson and myself, we were drinking buddies, as you can attest to.
